summary: Set 13 Session 2 (final session): Wired up the dabbler.copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt VS Code command, bumped extension to 0.12.1, built the VSIX, and ran end-of-session cross-provider verification. New file: tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt.ts (~21 lines, exports registerCopyAdoptionBootstrapPromptCommand which registers a single command that copies a constant ADOPTION_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT to clipboard via vscode.env.clipboard.writeText and shows an info message). Wired into src/extension.ts alongside the other registerXxx command groups. package.json: version 0.12.0 -> 0.12.1 (patch, additive command), description extended to mention 'adoption-bootstrap entry point', keywords appended with adoption/bootstrap/onboarding for Marketplace search, contributes.commands gained the new dabbler.copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt entry with title 'Copy adoption bootstrap prompt' and category 'Dabbler' (slotted between dabbler.getStarted and dabbler.setupNewProject). CHANGELOG.md: new v0.12.1 [2026-05-04] section under Unreleased, covering the new command + the four-tier budget-driven mode philosophy + the canonical doc URL, with a Notes block clarifying patch-vs-minor rationale and the explicit non-shipped automated-enforcement scope. tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/README.md: new 'Adoption bootstrap' subsection at the top of Features, one-paragraph form, with the canonical raw URL. Repo-root README.md: new 'For new projects: adoption bootstrap' top-level section inserted after 'Installing the VS Code extension' and before 'Adopting ai_router in a project', with both relative and absolute links to the canonical doc and a cross-link to docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md#cost-budgeted-verification-modes; entry added to the table of contents. Built dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.12.1.vsix (18 files, 331 KB) via npm run compile + npx vsce package; first build attempt was from stale dist/ because npx vsce package skips the compile step, caught and corrected via explicit npm run compile + repackage. Smoke test: VSIX manifest version=0.12.1, description includes 'adoption-bootstrap entry point', keywords include adoption/bootstrap/onboarding, the new command id appears in contributes.commands with the right title; bundled extension.js (285,149 bytes) contains the copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt source with the registration call and the URL string; round-trip-extracted ADOPTION_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT literal byte-matches the spec sketch; canonical URL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/darndestdabbler/dabbler-ai-orchestration/master/docs/adoption-bootstrap.md returns HTTP 200 (28879 bytes, matches Session 1). Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4): R1 VERIFIED with empty issues array on the first round, no fix rounds required. Cost: $0.1296 across two routed calls — $0.0898 on the first call (orchestrator's Python wrapper crashed reading a wrong RouteResult attribute name before capturing the verdict) + $0.0398 on the re-run that captured the VERIFIED verdict. Both calls logged to metrics.jsonl per the append-only contract. Set 013 close-out: this is the final session, so close-out writes change-log.md summarizing both sessions (cumulative spend $0.7194 vs spec projection $0.20-$0.55, 31% over driven entirely by Session 1's four-round verification surface). The 0.12.1 VSIX is committed alongside the source for sideload availability, matching the existing 0.12.0 VSIX artifact pattern.
